data.title: Lamb Weston: Solid Q3 Beat but International Headwinds and Strategic Uncertainty Justify Hold Rating
data.description:
Lamb Weston Holdings, the Consumer Defensive sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Robert Moskow from TD Cowen maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a $40.00 price target.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Robert Moskow has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Lamb Weston’s outlook and strategic uncertainty. While the company posted third-quarter results that exceeded expectations and modestly tightened its guidance, investor sentiment turned cautious as management highlighted exposure to the Middle East, intensifying competition, and the prospect of weaker pricing caused by lower crop values.

Moskow also underscores that the international segment is under meaningful pressure, with new capacity in key regions, high European potato yields, and subdued restaurant traffic weighing on demand and pricing power. The planned strategic review of the international portfolio, driven by new leadership and echoing activist investor recommendations, suggests potential restructuring that could ultimately create value but introduces execution risk, justifying a neutral stance at this time.

In another report released today, Exane BNP Paribas also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $41.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Encompass Health: Medicare Rate Proposal Reinforces Pricing Outlook and Supports Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Jared Haase has maintained their bullish stance on EHC stock, giving a Buy rating on March 30.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jared Haase has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to the latest Medicare payment proposal for inpatient rehabilitation facilities, which directly impacts Encompass Health. The proposed 2.4% rate update, built from a 3.2% market basket offset by a 0.8% productivity adjustment, essentially matches his prior expectations for the company’s pricing trajectory.

He notes that this proposal aligns closely with the assumptions already embedded in his financial model, which anticipated similar pricing growth for late 2026 and 2027, thereby reinforcing confidence in his forward estimates. While the final rule could modestly exceed the proposal based on past patterns, the current update alone is viewed as sufficiently constructive to support the existing outlook and justify maintaining a Buy recommendation on the shares.

Haase covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Aveanna Healthcare Holdings, Encompass Health, and US Physical Therapy. According to TipRanks, Haase has an average return of 1.5% and a 33.33% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on March 30, KeyBanc also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $145.00 price target.

data.title: Balanced Risk-Reward Amid Temporary Margin Pressures Justifies Hold on Chart Industries
data.description:
Chart Industries, the Industrials sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Marc Bianchi from TD Cowen maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a $210.00 price target.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Marc Bianchi has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Chart Industries’ recent results and outlook. The latest 4Q25 and 10-K filings highlighted softer profitability in the Specialty and CTS segments, alongside a much slower-than-anticipated conversion of existing backlog into 2026 revenue.

While he views the current margin pressure as largely temporary, linked to unique project wrap-ups and ongoing efficiency initiatives such as Teddy II, he expects profitability to gradually improve through 2026. However, with the price target maintained at $210, in line with the valuation implied by Baker Hughes’ acquisition price, the current risk‑reward appears balanced rather than compelling, justifying a Hold stance.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
